summary: Implemented Phase 3A. Renamed the temporary runtime/default faction id from steel_vanguard to kriegsia across protocol mirrors, rules/catalog data, client mirror, AI/sim fixtures, tests, and design docs. Added server-side lobby::faction_validation with context-specific default/accept/reject policy, wired it into lobby/start assembly plus replay, replay-branch, self-play, and dev-session validation, updated the lifecycle matrix, and marked plans/faction/phase-3a.md Done. Remaining steel_vanguard mentions are only migration notes in the faction plan/phase docs.
verification: cargo test --manifest-path server/Cargo.toml -p rts-server faction_validation: passed
cargo test --manifest-path server/Cargo.toml -p rts-rules faction: passed
cargo test --manifest-path server/Cargo.toml -p rts-sim replay_artifact: passed
gameplay_impact: No intended gameplay behavior change. Normal play still uses the existing faction, now canonically identified as kriegsia; ekaterina remains reserved in planning only and is not selectable or catalog-backed.
next_executor_notes: Phase 3B should build on server/src/lobby/faction_validation.rs for AI and prediction fail-closed policy. The fixture faction phase2_empty_fixture is accepted only by the TestFixture validation context; normal lobby, quickstart, AI, replay branch, replay playback, self-play, and dev-scenario contexts reject unsupported ids. Code/tests/design docs have no runtime steel_vanguard references.
manual_test_notes: Later manual sanity: start a normal local match and confirm lobby/start/gameplay still behave unchanged: current tech tree, starting City Centre/workers, resources, command card, AI add/start, and post-match replay startup.
